result.name: Batch ASCII STL Validation Report Generator
result.description: Validates a supplier batch of ASCII STL files for open edges, non-manifold edges, degenerate triangles, and inverted normals, then writes validation_report.json.

result.name: Improved ASCII STL Validator With Normal Consistency
result.description: Validates ASCII STL meshes for closure, non-manifold edges, degenerate triangles, and declared-normal consistency with vertex winding.

args.content:
import json
import subprocess
import sys

files = [f'binary_{i:03d}.stl' for i in range(1, 11)]
all_ok = True
for path in files:
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, 'stl_validator_binary_ascii.py', path], capture_output=True, text=True)
    print(path, p.returncode, p.stdout.strip())
    try:
        obj = json.loads(p.stdout)
        assert 'valid' in obj and 'issues' in obj
        assert isinstance(obj['issues'], list)
    except Exception as e:
        all_ok = False
        print('FAILED', path, repr(e))
if not all_ok:
    sys.exit(1)

manifest.name: Binary/ASCII STL Validator With Normal Consistency
manifest.description: Validates a single STL file in either binary or ASCII format for closure, non-manifold edges, degenerate triangles, and inverted declared normals.
